findings.description: Logos from the original document are missing in the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: The PDF contains the 'NCDENR' logo at the top and the 'One North Carolina Naturally' logo at the bottom. Neither image is present in the HTML rendering.

findings.description: Tabular layout in the header is flattened into sequential text.
findings.evidence: The PDF displays the names and titles (Governor, Director, Secretary) in three distinct, horizontally aligned columns. The HTML renders these as stacked lines of text ('Beverly Eaves Perdue Governor', etc.).

findings.description: Bracketed text lost line breaks, indentation, and italic formatting.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, bracketed text like '[Authority for this condition...]' is italicized and placed on a new, indented line below its corresponding paragraph. In the HTML, it is rendered as plain text appended directly to the end of the preceding paragraph without a line break.

findings.description: Ordered lists are rendered as run-on paragraphs.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, section 'D' has a lettered list (a. through g.) and section 'E' has a lettered list (a. through h.) with each item on a new line. In the HTML, these list items are merged into single, continuous paragraphs.

findings.description: Sub-list and trailing contact block are rendered as run-on paragraphs/lines.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the bullet for GC3819 contains a lettered sub-list (a, b, c) on separate lines. The HTML flattens this into a single paragraph. Additionally, the 'cc:' block at the bottom of the PDF lists names on individual lines, which the HTML flattens into one continuous string.

findings.description: The document lacks semantic HTML heading tags (h1-h6) to represent the document's structure, relying instead on bolded text within paragraph tags.
findings.evidence:
Section titles such as '

I. Umbrella Mitigation Banking Instrument (UMBI) Template for Nutrient Offset Mitigation Banks

' and '

II. Proposed Water Quality Certification...

' are styled with strong tags rather than appropriate

tags.
